reviews.review_title: It not scary or is it just me..
reviews.review_description: I got this game I got in and no I did not scream at the top of my lungs,i didn’t just stand for it to get me . I ran fast. I am already In level 5 , can we just talk about the doll that they move places , the clown was at the table then to the floor , the tall dolls were on the top of the closet one of them was sitting on the chair and the tallest one was sitting in the corner and moved next to the door.. overall it’s not that scary, I mean to me it’s not but for some people are . I get it what makes it scary . Dolls move to random places, monster is a doll , you have to beat the game, you have to find dolls , you will meet different monsters in the game , I feel like I am just typing a essay about this 💀
Rarely get jump scared but JEEZ this game is on another level

The game overall is good and bad, butfinding all the dolls to complete it is okay. The game does take some time which also gives you a heart attack every time you hear, baby cry, and is very fun to make someone play the game and watch them scream!

This really did not need to be a rip off of Five Nights At Freddy's. It makes zero sense. The crawling concept is great but everything else is in dire need of work. Normally I'd not judge an App Lab game harshly but considering the dev is already making "Crawlspace 2" without this one leaving App Lab, it would seem to me that a proper finished game is unlikely.

If dolls creep you out, this game is your worst nightmare. I played the first level and then had to uninstall the game because I just couldn't. There might be jump scares in later levels, but I didn't even make it that far. I will try again when there are people around me to keep me from having an anxiety attack mid-game. 

There's a bit of jankiness to the game, but frankly, I kind of like it because it gives it an early 2000's vibe to it like the first Silent Hill games.

So I finally beat this game after getting through the first couple of levels when I downloaded it like 6 months ago, dropping it for 5 months and beating another level so at this point I’m on level 5 so I’m like “Ok this is it just one more level and I’m done, I can finally stop” so I get in the room and realize that 1# not one, not two, but three of the babies are gone so that’s fun and 2# all of the bad dolls that try to you know kill you so I’m like yea I’m good and drop it again for another month. So as I was playing, I was sharing to my tv and my family was watching and apparently they like watching me suffer so I got peer pressured into getting back on the game and beating the last level so I get on and on my first try I like speedran right to every doll right away and was done in like under 2 minutes and I’m done so like the whole ending thing happens it makes me sign a thing, the game kicks me out and yea it’s done so I go back in the game just to see if anything else happened and when I got back in the tv told me that I could use the calendar to teleport to any day and play that day again so I thought it would be cool just to see what day one was like after beating the game so I go in and beat the tutorial and when I leave the room and go back to the lobby thing and I look at the calendar to teleport to another day and guess what I can’t I’m stuck at day two again and I’m never gonna play this game and do all that again so thanks devs it would be cool if you could put me back if you can even do that or just get it fixed so you can always go to whatever level you want when you win but all in all pretty good game scared the liquid poop out of me a couple times oh and also you should add multiplayer and proximity chat that would be pretty cool.

reviews.review_title: not as scary as you think
reviews.review_description: this game is trash. get it, play it… but you won’t enjoy it. i got this expecting a HORRIFYING game, but all I got was this game. im HORRIFIED of dolls, but this just ain’t scary.

I typically don't play those games designed to be scary with jump scares, loud noises, and strange happenings, but I took a chance on this game.  

I have had it for months and still have not finished the game.  Whatever you do, do NOT play this game alone, late at night, because that will play games with your mind. 

Good luck crawling under the house to find the spooky dolls in the tight locations.  This game still has a long way to go before it is ready for the mainstream game page, but when it does be ready to have the sh*t scared out of you!

The simplicity of creating pure scares in this game is amazing. 

This is a win. 

But…

(I hope the dev responds to this with his/her thoughts)

But I feel the movement mechanics can be even better by a simple addition:

Allowing turning with just your hands/grip buttons, not the toggle.

Think of how someone turns a bicycle using the handlebars. Use the same direction mechanics. 

As of the current state of the game, when you crawl through in a straight forward direction, you grab the dirt with the grip button with one controller and pull towards you and then let go and then repeat with the other controller. While doing this you turn by using the right controllers toggle….. which can get a little fumble’y.

I think it would be so so so much easier (and feel more natural) when you make turns if you grab the dirt with both hands at the same time  (regardless where they are placed in the dirt) and pull the right hand towards you to turn right and vice versa to turn left (or pull the right controller towards you to go left or left controller to go right if that seems easier). Then let go of one of the grip triggers to get back to the normal mechanics of straight forward movement. 

In other words, pivot left and right by means of holding onto the dirt with both hands and pulling one of them towards you depending which direction you want to go. =)

reviews.review_title: Scarier than Five Nights at Freddy's
reviews.review_description:
Video footage of me playing Crawlspace for the first time: https://youtu.be/WPbRSjDJS6M

This is one of the best horror games I've played in a while, it creeped me out even more than Five Nights At Freddy's! I think it's the added element of being in that tiny crawlspace, plus that scuttling noise shortly before they get you....great work! I'm really impressed that the developer gave it away for free too!
